'''Michael Tandre''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] serving the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].

==Biography==
Michael Tandre was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and [[Geologist/Legends|geologist]]. Over two hundred [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Tandre crashed his [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Alpha Kentrum]]'' one the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Orellon II]]. Surviving the crash, Tandre encountered the world's [[Kentra|indigenous species]] and was welcomed into their society. Tandre served as a spiritual leader to the species and accepted that he was stranded indefinitely and began to teach the natives the principles of [[Sword, Plow, and Spirit]]. Crafting a series of computer programs from the ''Alpha Kentrum''{{'s}} wreckage, he hoped to preserve his message should he [[Death/Legends|die]] or be rescued. His followers, calling themselves the [[Holy Order of the Je'ulajists]], based on a mispronunciation of "geologist", tended to their spiritual guide throughout the rest of his life.<ref name="TP" />

While rescue never came for Tandre, the species, calling themselves the Kentra, preserved his teaching as the basis of their society. During the Galactic Civil War, a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] agent searching for a potential [[Safe world/Legends|safe world]] fixed the computers that Tandre set up and discovered his final resting place, the wreck of the ''Alpha Kentrum'', and Michael Tandre's [[Color/Legends|orange]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] known as the "[[Sword of Honor]]," which he left for his [[Kentra]] disciples on [[Orellon II]].<ref name="TP" />
